title: Indonesia Plans First Nuclear Power Plant by 2034, Eyes Partnerships with Russia and Canada
body: The proposed facility is designed to have a capacity of up to 500 megawatts and will be developed across Sumatra and Kalimantan.

title: Indonesia Urged to Act on $8 Billion Low-Carbon Fuel Export Opportunity
body: Eurocham expert urges early investment to capitalize on global demand and incentives.

title: Garuda Indonesia Targets 120 Aircraft by 2030 Following $407M Capital Boost
body: Garuda operated 144 aircraft prior to the COVID-19 pandemic, but that number dropped to fewer than 40 after a debt restructuring in 2022.

title: Indonesia Reassesses $24 Billion Refinery Project with Russia’s Rosneft
body: Seven years after launch, the Pertamina-Rosneft project in East Java faces an investment review over limited progress

title: Garuda Indonesia Secures $405 Million Loan from Danantara
body: Garuda Indonesia will use the shareholder loan from Danantara for maintenance and inspection work.

title: BRMS Underground Gold Mine to Triple Output by 2027
body: BRMS to open its first underground gold mine in Palu by 2027, aiming to triple output as Q1 profit surges 296% on strong gold sales.

title: Indonesia’s Palm Oil Export Drops 39 Pct as EU Buys Less
body: Indonesia only exported around 1.78 million tons of palm oil products in April, down 39.2 percent from the previous month.

title: Indonesia Delays Sugary Drink Tax to 2026
body: Indonesia delays sugary drink tax to 2026 due to regulatory and economic concerns, seeking other revenue to meet 2025 targets.

title: Pertamina Says It Has Enough Fuel Supplies as Iran Threatens to Block Hormuz
body: The Iranian parliament has given its nod to shut the Strait of Hormuz after the US bombed the country's its nuclear sites.

title: Indonesia Plans Nuclear Plants with Potential 24,000 Tons of Uranium in Reserve
body: Indonesia is drafting regulations to manage uranium in Kalimantan as it eyes nuclear power to meet future energy demands.

title: Analyst Warns Oil Prices Could Surpass $100 as Iran-Israel Tensions Escalate
body: While the baseline for global oil prices has hovered around $70 per barrel, current geopolitical risks could double that figure.

title: Over 20,000 Live Cattle Imported from Australia to Boost Indonesia’s Meat and Dairy Output
body: The government has lifted restrictions on live cattle imports and offers incentives to boost domestic meat and dairy production.

title: Indonesia Predicted to Grow Just 4.5 Pct This Year if Israel-Iran War Doesn’t Stop
body: Analyst Bhima Yudhistira also says the looming Strait of Hormuz closure will cause oil prices to soar.

title: Indonesia Seeks Joint Venture Fertilizer Plant with Russia
body: Indonesia would increase crude palm oil (CPO) exports to Russia, while in return, Russia would facilitate beef exports to Indonesia.

title: Oil Rises and US Stock Futures Slip as Markets React to US Strike on Iran Nuclear Sites
body: Iran could severely disrupt transit through it, sending insurance rates spiking and making shippers nervous to move without US Navy escorts.

title: Jakarta-Owned Bank Rebrands as Bank Jakarta, Eyes IPO in 2026
body: The rebranding comes as Jakarta prepares to relinquish status as Indonesia’s capital, which will relocate to Nusantara, East Kalimantan.

title: Indonesia Adds a Population the Size of Singapore Every Year, Prabowo Tells Russian Forum
body: President highlights food, energy, and education reforms as pillars of national strategy at St. Petersburg economic gathering.

title: Indonesia Weighs China, Russia Nuclear Tech as 24,000-Ton Uranium Reserve Identified
body: Indonesia may use Chinese or Russian tech for its first nuclear plants, with 24,000 tons of uranium in Kalimantan ready to fuel the plan.

title: Gov’t Finds No Major Damage, Lets Gag Nikel Resume Raja Ampat Mining
body: Indonesia allows Gag Nikel to resume mining in Raja Ampat after finding its environmental management meets required standards.

title: Danantara, Himbara Banks Back $8 Billion Housing Finance Program
body: Indonesia will channel Rp 130 trillion ($8B) in housing loans via new state fund Danantara, backed by major state-owned banks.

title: Russia to Export More Wheat, Meat to Indonesia
body: Indonesia and Russia have previously agreed to recognize each other's halal certification, thus paving the way for the meat trade.

title: Danantara, Russia's RDIF to Launch $2.3 Billion Investment Platform
body: Russian Direct Investment Fund (RDIF) officially joins the list of Danantara's foreign partners.

title: Russia Ready to Boost Oil Supplies to Indonesia, Putin Tells Prabowo
body: Putin tells Prabowo that Russia is ready to ramp up oil and LNG supplies to Indonesia.

title: Indonesia’s Bumi Resources to Acquire Australian Gold and Copper Miner Wolfram Limited
body: The acquisition is Bumi's strategic move to expand into critical minerals amid growing global demand for green energy transition metals.

title: Morowali Industrial Park Pledges Reforms After Environmental Warning from Minister
body: Despite the setback, Dedy stressed that IMIP uses advanced technology to reduce emissions from its nickel smelters.

title: Russia's Economy Minister Says the Country Is on 'The Brink of Recession'
body: High defense spending has propelled growth and kept unemployment low in Russia despite fueling inflation.

title: Indonesia’s Biggest Solar Panel Factory Begins Operation in Kendal SEZ
body: Indonesia opens its largest solar panel factory in Kendal SEZ to drive renewable energy, backed by Trina Solar and Sinarmas Group.

title: Indonesia Drops 13 Places in IMD's Global Competitiveness Ranking
body: Indonesia slips 13 places to 40th in the 2025 World Competitiveness Ranking, falling behind Malaysia amid infrastructure and policy gaps.

title: PLN Posts $33b Revenue in 2024, But Profit Drops 19.5%
body: PLN booked Rp545.4t in revenue for 2024, up 11.9% from last year, while net profit dropped 19.5% to Rp17.76t

title: Minister Defends Controversial Mini-Home Design, Says No Final Decision Made
body: The proposed design calls for subsidized homes as small as 18 square meters of building space on 25 square meters of land.

title: Bank Indonesia Holds Benchmark Rate at 5.5 Pct
body: Bank Indonesia keeps key rate at 5.5%, citing stable inflation and rupiah.

title: Danantara Redefines How Indonesia Funds SOEs
body: Danantara reshapes SOE funding by reinvesting Rp150t in dividends into strategic projects, ending reliance on direct state budget support.

title: Trump’s Tariffs Trigger Sharp Drop in Japan’s US Auto Shipments
body: Japan logs $4.4B trade deficit in May as US auto tariffs imposed by Trump slash car exports by nearly 25%, deepening economic strain.

title: Indonesia, Pertamina on Alert as Iran-Israel Conflict Drives Up Oil Prices
body: Indonesia braces for economic fallout as the Iran-Israel war pushes oil prices higher; Pertamina ramps up monitoring of supply routes.

title: Indonesia’s Government Debt Jumps 164% to $21b in 5 Months
body: Indonesia’s government debt hits Rp 349.3T in Jan–May 2025, up 164% YoY, signaling mounting fiscal pressure despite surplus claims.

title: Iran Wants Indonesia to Boycott Israeli Goods as War Rages On
body: Iran says Muslim nations like Indonesia must not stop at declarations, but also to exert economic pressure on Israel.

title: Danantara, INA Invest in Chandra Asri's $800m Chemical Plant
body: Danantara, INA, and Chandra Asri invest $800M in CA-EDC plant to slash imports, boost exports, and strengthen Indonesia's industrial base.

title: Prabowo Says Danantara Copies Singapore’s $303 Billion Temasek ‘With Pride’
body: Prabowo admits to having copied Singapore’s Temasek while hoping to strike a Danantara deal.

title: BCA Reports Rp 25 Trillion in Net Profit Through May 2025, Driven by Dividends and Cost Efficiency
body: Even without factoring in dividends, BCA’s net profit still posted a solid 10.12 percent year-on-year increase.
